request.question: Both Should Have Known Better and Carrie & Lowell have which instrumentalist playing on them?

title: The Broadside Tapes 1
Richard Nunns
I Should Have Known Better
U Should've Known Better
Ahmed Zuway
Scott Lucas (musician)
Pilipili
Should Have Known Better
Malcolm Laycock
Carrie & Lowell
sentences: The Broadside Tapes 1, alternatively known as "Broadside Ballads, Vol.
14", was a compilation of demo recordings done by Phil Ochs for "Broadside" magazine in the early-to-late 1960s.
Of the sixteen songs that appeared, ranging from the humorous ("The Ballad of Alferd Packer") to the depressing ("The Passing of My Life"), all were new to listeners.

It also included a song about the Profumo affair ("Christine Keeler") and it closed with a live cover of The Beatles' "I Should Have Known Better" (retitled "I Shoulda Known Better") featuring Eric Andersen on harmony vocals and harmonica.
Richard Nunns QSM (born 1945) is a Māori traditional instrumentalist of Pākehā heritage.
He is particularly known for playing the Taonga pūoro and his collaboration with fellow Māori instrumentalist Hirini Melbourne.
Since Melbourne's death, he is regarded as the world's foremost authority on Māori instruments.

"I Should Have Known Better" is a song by English rock band the Beatles composed by John Lennon (credited to Lennon–McCartney), and originally issued on "A Hard Day's Night", their soundtrack for the film of the same name released July 10, 1964.
"I Should Have Known Better" was issued as the B-side of the U.S. single "A Hard Day's Night" released July 13, 1964.
An orchestrated version of the song conducted by George Martin appears on the North American version of the album, "A Hard Day's Night Original Motion Picture Soundtrack."
"U Should've Known Better" is a song by American recording artist Monica.

It was written in collaboration with Harold Lilly and Jermaine Dupri, and produced by the latter along with frequent co-producer Bryan Michael Cox for her original third studio album, "All Eyez on Me" (2002).
When the album was shelved for release outside Japan, the song was one out of five original records that were transferred into its new version, "After the Storm" (2003).
A contemporary R&B slow jam, "U Should've Known Better" contains elements of soul music and rock music.
Built on an pulsating backbeat, the song's instrumentation consists of screeching guitars and an understated harp pattern.
Lyrically, Monica, as the protagonist, delivers a message of loyalty to her imprisoned love interest and sings about staying down for him despite his doubts.
Ahmed Mahmoud Zuway (Arabic: أحمد الزوي‎ ‎ ) (born December 28, 1982), better known better as Kabila, is a Libyan football striker.
He currently plays for Ahly Benghazi in the Libyan Premier League.
He is a member of the Libyan national football team.
He is known for his excellent heading ability and goal scoring.

David Scott Lucas (born May 10, 1970), known better as Scott Lucas, is an American musician, who is best known for being the guitarist, bassist, and lead vocalist for Local H, as well as the lead vocalist and guitarist of Scott Lucas and the Married Men.
Peter Gatonye (born November 1982), known better for his stage name Pilipili is a musician/singer from Kenya.
At the beginning of his career, he collaborated with another musician, Gun B.
Their best known song was "Nampenda".
He has also sung a song with Ratatat.
The song was called Morale.
"Should Have Known Better" is a song by American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Sufjan Stevens.
It is the second track and second single from his seventh studio album, "Carrie & Lowell", and was released digitally on March 11, 2015 on Asthmatic Kitty.
A promotional CD was later released on Asthmatic Kitty but was not available for sale.
Malcolm Richard Laycock (1 November 1938 – 8 November 2009) was a schoolteacher until moving into broadcasting, initially on Radio London.
He is now known better as a British radio presenter and producer, best known for his work on programmes related to jazz, dance band and big band music.
During his career he presented shows for both BBC Radio 2 and the BBC World Service.
Between 1995 and 2009, he was also the host of "Sunday Night at 10", a long running weekly Sunday evening show on Radio 2.
Carrie & Lowell is the seventh studio album by American musician Sufjan Stevens, released through Asthmatic Kitty on March 31, 2015.
Unlike Stevens's previous studio album, the electronic "The Age of Adz", "Carrie & Lowell" is sparsely instrumental and marks a return to the performer's indie folk roots.
The album was given a one-week preview on NPR.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Both Should Have Known Better and Carrie & Lowell have which instrumentalist playing on them?
hop1_docs: Should Have Known Better | "Should Have Known Better" is a song by American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Sufjan Stevens.  It is the second track and second single from his seventh studio album, "Carrie & Lowell", and was released digitally on March 11, 2015 on Asthmatic Kitty.  A promotional CD was later released on Asthmatic Kitty but was not available for sale.
I Should Have Known Better (disambiguation) | "I Should Have Known Better" is a song by The Beatles.

I Should Have Known Better | "I Should Have Known Better" is a song by English rock band the Beatles composed by John Lennon (credited to Lennon–McCartney), and originally issued on "A Hard Day's Night", their soundtrack for the film of the same name released July 10, 1964.  "I Should Have Known Better" was issued as the B-side of the U.S. single "A Hard Day's Night" released July 13, 1964.  An orchestrated version of the song conducted by George Martin appears on the North American version of the album, "A Hard Day's Night Original Motion Picture Soundtrack."

I Should Have Known Better (Jim Diamond song) | "I Should Have Known Better" was a UK number one single for one week in December 1984 for Jim Diamond.  The song was displaced after one week by Frankie Goes to Hollywood's song "The Power of Love".  Diamond publicly requested that people not buy his single, but instead buy "Do They Know It's Christmas? "

Homolidae | The family Homolidae, known as carrier crabs or porter crabs, contains 14 genera of marine crabs.  They mostly live on the continental slope and continental shelf, and are rarely encountered.  Members of the Homolidae have their fifth pereiopods (last pair of walking legs) in a sub-dorsal position, which allows them to hold objects in place over the rear half of the carapace.  The objects carried include sponges, black corals and gorgonians, and this behaviour may be a defence mechanism against predators.  Some species have been observed carrying living sea urchins in a symbiotic relationship which allows them to benefit from the protection of the urchin's dangerous spikes.

Armies in the American Civil War | This article is designed to give background into the organization and tactics of Civil War armies.  This brief survey is by no means exhaustive, but it should give enough material to have a better understanding of the capabilities of the forces that fought the American Civil War.  Understanding these capabilities should give insight into the reasoning behind the decisions made by commanders on both sides.

Jim Diamond (singer) | James Aaron Diamond (28 September 1951 – 8 October 2015) was a Scottish singer-songwriter, best known for his three Top 5 hits.  The first was "I Won't Let You Down" (1982), as the lead singer in the trio PhD, with Tony Hymas and Simon Phillips.  His solo performance, "I Should Have Known Better", was a United Kingdom No.1 in 1984.  The third track was the theme song from "Boon", "Hi Ho Silver" which reached No.5 in the UK Singles Chart in 1986.  He has also featured as a vocalist on the charity No.1s "You'll Never Walk Alone" with The Crowd and "Let It Be" with Ferry Aid.  His last UK chart success was with "Young Love (Carry Me Away)" in 1986.
hop1_titles: I Should Have Known Better (Jim Diamond song)
Armies in the American Civil War
response.summary_1:
Primary topics: the song "Should Have Known Better" and the album "Carrie & Lowell".

- "Should Have Known Better" — "a song by ... Sufjan Stevens" and "the second track and second single from his seventh studio album, 'Carrie & Lowell'."  
- Sufjan Stevens — described as an "American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist."  
- Missing/ambiguous facts: the passages do not list any other session musicians or a separate credited instrumentalist; no explicit "Personnel" or credits for the album/song are provided (bridge entity: the album "Carrie & Lowell" and the song pages).  
- Next-hop retrieval (priority order):  
  1) Fetch "Should Have Known Better (song)" page — inspect lede/infobox and "Personnel" or "Credits" section.  
  2) Fetch "Carrie & Lowell" (album) page — inspect lede/infobox and "Personnel" or "Credits" section.  
  3) Fetch "Sufjan Stevens" page — inspect lede/infobox and "Instrument(s)"/career sections to confirm instruments credited.  

No additional retrieval needed to identify that Sufjan Stevens is the multi-instrumentalist named in the passages; further retrieval would confirm other credited instrumentalists.

No Shade in the Shadow of the Cross | "No Shade in the Shadow of the Cross" is a song by American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist Sufjan Stevens.  It is the tenth track and lead single from his seventh studio album, "Carrie & Lowell", and was released digitally on February 16, 2015 on Asthmatic Kitty and as a 7" on March 3, 2015 on Joyful Noise Recordings.

Carrie & Lowell Tour | The Carrie & Lowell Tour was the sixth concert tour by American recording artist Sufjan Stevens.  The tour supported his seventh studio album, "Carrie & Lowell" (2015).  The tour began in the spring of 2015.  It played over 100 shows in North America, Australasia and Europe.

Carrie & Lowell | Carrie & Lowell is the seventh studio album by American musician Sufjan Stevens, released through Asthmatic Kitty on March 31, 2015. response.summary_2: Sufjan Stevens — described as an "American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist." Sufjan Stevens appears on both: "Should Have Known Better" is a song by him and "Carrie & Lowell" is his seventh studio album.
